mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, causing investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real consumer spending indicate a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of historical instances where both series decline concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, each 25 basis point increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity valuations by raising the risk-free rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive monthly rate hikes exceeding 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence, which boosts forward-looking equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of months where housing starts increase month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 100.0, it indicates severe supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that suppress corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production remains below 100.0 for consecutive months while S&P 500 forward 60-day returns average above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining real consumer spending signals a wage-price spiral breakdown and weakening aggregate demand, which forces equity risk premiums higher and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration of labor and consumer spending metrics fails to correlate with negative 20-day equity returns in more than 55% of observed episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, a month-over-month decline in real consumer spending accelerates the Federal Reserve's hawkish pivot, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Real consumer spending declines while CPI remains above 8.0 percent, yet S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances.
